documentation:
## Aliyun ROS WAF3 Construct Library

This module is part of the AliCloud ROS Cloud Development Kit (ROS CDK) project.

```python
import * as WAF3 from '@alicloud/ros-cdk-waf3';
```

classes.name: ros_cdk_waf3.Instance

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.Instance.__init__
documentation:
Param scope - scope in which this resource is defined Param id - scoped id of the resource Param props - resource properties.

```
param scope



param id



param props



param enable_resource_property_constraint

```

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.Instance.attr_instance_id
documentation: Attribute InstanceId: Instance Id.

This class encapsulates and extends the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::WAF3::Instance`, which is used to create a Web Application Firewall (WAF) 3.0 instance.

```
Note

```

This class may have some new functions to facilitate development, so it is recommended to use this class instead of RosInstance\`\`for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-waf3-instance>

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.__init__
documentation:
Properties for defining a `Instance`.

See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-waf3-instance>

```
param pay_type

Property payType: The billing method of the firewall instance. Valid values: PayAsYouGo: pay-as-you-go Subscription: subscription

param region

Property region: Web Application Firewall is available in the following regions: regions in the Chinese mainland, China (Hong Kong), Singapore (Singapore), Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur), US (Silicon Valley), Australia (Sydney), Germany (Frankfurt), India (Mumbai), Indonesia (Jakarta), UAE (Dubai), and Japan (Tokyo). If your origin server is deployed within the Chinese mainland, select Chinese Mainland. If your origin server is deployed outside the Chinese mainland, select Outside Chinese mainland. Intelligent region selection is supported.

param additional_protection_nodes

Property additionalProtectionNodes: Each protection cluster has at least two protection nodes, and each node provides the protection capabilities of up to 5,000 QPS for HTTP requests or up to 3,000 QPS for HTTPS requests. You can add protection nodes to increase protection capabilities.

param api_security

Property apiSecurity: The API security feature detects responses with specified characteristics to check whether data leaks occur. After you enable the feature, WAF is authorized to perform related analysis on your data. If you select Chinese Mainland, service deployment and data processing are performed in the Chinese mainland.

param auto_pay

Property autoPay: Whether to auto pay the bill.

param auto_renew

Property autoRenew: Whether to auto renew the prepay instance.

param bot_app_protection

Property botAppProtection: Bot management module for App protection.

param bot_web_protection

Property botWebProtection: Bot management module for Web application protection.

param domains_extension

Property domainsExtension: If the actual number of required access domain names exceeds the number of free domain names in the version, the number of domain names can be expanded according to this specification.Domain name counting does not differentiate between domain name types. The main domain name, sub-domain name, and pan-domain name are each counted as one domain name.

param elastic_qps

Property elasticQps: The burstable QPS (pay-as-you-go) feature is suitable for scenarios that involve short-term or sudden traffic surges, for example, during promotions. In these scenarios, the traffic peak may exceed the sum of the maximum QPS that is supported by your WAF edition and the extended QPS. If you enable the feature, you are charged based on the amount of excess QPS resources that you use. This helps prevent your domain names from being added to a sandbox when QPS resources are excessively used and helps ensure service continuity.

param exclusive_ip_address

Property exclusiveIpAddress: Excluesive IP address number.

param fraud_detection

Property fraudDetection: You can enable this feature only after you enable the bot management module. If abnormal phone numbers are used in logon or registration scenarios, anomaly tags are matched. Requests from the abnormal phone numbers are blocked or CAPTCHA verification is required. You are charged based on the number of times that anomaly tags are matched.

param ignore_existing

Property ignoreExisting: Whether to ignore existing WAF3 instance False: ROS will perform a uniqueness check.If the WAF3 instance exists, an error will be reported when creating it. True: ROS will not check the uniqueness.If the WAF3 instance exists, the creation process will be ignored. If the WAF3 instance is not created by ROS, it will be ignored during update and delete stage.

param intelligent_load_balancing

Property intelligentLoadBalancing: Intelligent load balancer for WAF instance.

param log_service

Property logService: Log service for WAF instance.

param log_storage

Property logStorage: Log storage capacity.

param period

Property period: The subscription period of the firewallIf PeriodUnit is month, the valid range is 1, 3, 6 If periodUnit is year, the valid range is 1, 2, 3.

param period_unit

Property periodUnit: The unit of the subscription duration. Valid values: Month Year Default value: Month.

param qps_extension

Property qpsExtension: Extended QPS.

param traffic_billing_protection_threshold

Property trafficBillingProtectionThreshold: In pay-as-you-go WAF 3.0, the traffic billing protection feature is automatically enabled to prevent unexpected and unusually high bills that result from unpredictable factors such as HTTP flood attacks. A bill is not generated for an hour if the peak traffic exceeds the traffic billing protection threshold within the hour. Then, your WAF instance is added to a sandbox. If the peak traffic is lower than the traffic billing protection threshold the next hour, your WAF instance is removed from the sandbox.

param waf_version

Property wafVersion: The version of WAF3.0.
```

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.pay_type
documentation:
Property payType: The billing method of the firewall instance.

Valid values:
PayAsYouGo: pay-as-you-go
Subscription: subscription

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.region
documentation:
Property region: Web Application Firewall is available in the following regions: regions in the Chinese mainland, China (Hong Kong), Singapore (Singapore), Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur), US (Silicon Valley), Australia (Sydney), Germany (Frankfurt), India (Mumbai), Indonesia (Jakarta), UAE (Dubai), and Japan (Tokyo).

If your origin server is deployed within the Chinese mainland, select Chinese Mainland. If your origin server is deployed outside the Chinese mainland, select Outside Chinese mainland. Intelligent region selection is supported.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.additional_protection_nodes
documentation:
Property additionalProtectionNodes: Each protection cluster has at least two protection nodes, and each node provides the protection capabilities of up to 5,000 QPS for HTTP requests or up to 3,000 QPS for HTTPS requests.

You can add protection nodes to increase protection capabilities.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.api_security
documentation:
Property apiSecurity: The API security feature detects responses with specified characteristics to check whether data leaks occur.

After you enable the feature, WAF is authorized to perform related analysis on your data. If you select Chinese Mainland, service deployment and data processing are performed in the Chinese mainland.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.auto_pay
documentation: Property autoPay: Whether to auto pay the bill.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.auto_renew
documentation: Property autoRenew: Whether to auto renew the prepay instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.bot_app_protection
documentation: Property botAppProtection: Bot management module for App protection.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.bot_web_protection
documentation: Property botWebProtection: Bot management module for Web application protection.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.domains_extension
documentation: Property domainsExtension: If the actual number of required access domain names exceeds the number of free domain names in the version, the number of domain names can be expanded according to this specification.Domain name counting does not differentiate between domain name types. The main domain name, sub-domain name, and pan-domain name are each counted as one domain name.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.elastic_qps
documentation:
Property elasticQps: The burstable QPS (pay-as-you-go) feature is suitable for scenarios that involve short-term or sudden traffic surges, for example, during promotions.

In these scenarios, the traffic peak may exceed the sum of the maximum QPS that is supported by your WAF edition and the extended QPS. If you enable the feature, you are charged based on the amount of excess QPS resources that you use. This helps prevent your domain names from being added to a sandbox when QPS resources are excessively used and helps ensure service continuity.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.exclusive_ip_address
documentation: Property exclusiveIpAddress: Excluesive IP address number.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.fraud_detection
documentation:
Property fraudDetection: You can enable this feature only after you enable the bot management module.

If abnormal phone numbers are used in logon or registration scenarios, anomaly tags are matched. Requests from the abnormal phone numbers are blocked or CAPTCHA verification is required. You are charged based on the number of times that anomaly tags are matched.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.ignore_existing
documentation: Property ignoreExisting: Whether to ignore existing WAF3 instance False: ROS will perform a uniqueness check.If the WAF3 instance exists, an error will be reported when creating it. True: ROS will not check the uniqueness.If the WAF3 instance exists, the creation process will be ignored. If the WAF3 instance is not created by ROS, it will be ignored during update and delete stage.

documentation: Property intelligentLoadBalancing: Intelligent load balancer for WAF instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.InstanceProps.traffic_billing_protection_threshold
documentation: Property trafficBillingProtectionThreshold: In pay-as-you-go WAF 3.0, the traffic billing protection feature is automatically enabled to prevent unexpected and unusually high bills that result from unpredictable factors such as HTTP flood attacks. A bill is not generated for an hour if the peak traffic exceeds the traffic billing protection threshold within the hour. Then, your WAF instance is added to a sandbox. If the peak traffic is lower than the traffic billing protection threshold the next hour, your WAF instance is removed from the sandbox.

documentation: :Property: trafficBillingProtectionThreshold: In pay-as-you-go WAF 3.0, the traffic billing protection feature is automatically enabled to prevent unexpected and unusually high bills that result from unpredictable factors such as HTTP flood attacks. A bill is not generated for an hour if the peak traffic exceeds the traffic billing protection threshold within the hour. Then, your WAF instance is added to a sandbox. If the peak traffic is lower than the traffic billing protection threshold the next hour, your WAF instance is removed from the sandbox.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstance.waf_version
documentation: :Property: wafVersion: The version of WAF3.0.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::WAF3::Instance`, which is used to create a Web Application Firewall (WAF) 3.0 instance.

```
Note

```

This class does not contain additional functions, so it is recommended to use the `Instance` class instead of this class for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-waf3-instance>

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.pay_type
documentation:
:Property:

payType: The billing method of the firewall instance. Valid values:
PayAsYouGo: pay-as-you-go
Subscription: subscription

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.region
documentation:
:Property:

region: Web Application Firewall is available in the following regions: regions in the Chinese mainland, China (Hong Kong), Singapore (Singapore), Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur), US (Silicon Valley), Australia (Sydney), Germany (Frankfurt), India (Mumbai), Indonesia (Jakarta), UAE (Dubai), and Japan (Tokyo).
If your origin server is deployed within the Chinese mainland, select Chinese Mainland. If your origin server is deployed outside the Chinese mainland, select Outside Chinese mainland. Intelligent region selection is supported.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.additional_protection_nodes
documentation: :Property: additionalProtectionNodes: Each protection cluster has at least two protection nodes, and each node provides the protection capabilities of up to 5,000 QPS for HTTP requests or up to 3,000 QPS for HTTPS requests. You can add protection nodes to increase protection capabilities.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.api_security
documentation: :Property: apiSecurity: The API security feature detects responses with specified characteristics to check whether data leaks occur. After you enable the feature, WAF is authorized to perform related analysis on your data. If you select Chinese Mainland, service deployment and data processing are performed in the Chinese mainland.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.auto_pay
documentation: :Property: autoPay: Whether to auto pay the bill.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.auto_renew
documentation: :Property: autoRenew: Whether to auto renew the prepay instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.bot_app_protection
documentation: :Property: botAppProtection: Bot management module for App protection.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.bot_web_protection
documentation: :Property: botWebProtection: Bot management module for Web application protection.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.domains_extension
documentation: :Property: domainsExtension: If the actual number of required access domain names exceeds the number of free domain names in the version, the number of domain names can be expanded according to this specification.Domain name counting does not differentiate between domain name types. The main domain name, sub-domain name, and pan-domain name are each counted as one domain name.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.elastic_qps
documentation: :Property: elasticQps: The burstable QPS (pay-as-you-go) feature is suitable for scenarios that involve short-term or sudden traffic surges, for example, during promotions. In these scenarios, the traffic peak may exceed the sum of the maximum QPS that is supported by your WAF edition and the extended QPS. If you enable the feature, you are charged based on the amount of excess QPS resources that you use. This helps prevent your domain names from being added to a sandbox when QPS resources are excessively used and helps ensure service continuity.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.exclusive_ip_address
documentation: :Property: exclusiveIpAddress: Excluesive IP address number.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.fraud_detection
documentation: :Property: fraudDetection: You can enable this feature only after you enable the bot management module. If abnormal phone numbers are used in logon or registration scenarios, anomaly tags are matched. Requests from the abnormal phone numbers are blocked or CAPTCHA verification is required. You are charged based on the number of times that anomaly tags are matched.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.ignore_existing
documentation:
:Property:

ignoreExisting: Whether to ignore existing WAF3 instance
False: ROS will perform a uniqueness check.If the WAF3 instance exists, an error will be reported when creating it.
True: ROS will not check the uniqueness.If the WAF3 instance exists, the creation process will be ignored.
If the WAF3 instance is not created by ROS, it will be ignored during update and delete stage.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.intelligent_load_balancing
documentation: :Property: intelligentLoadBalancing: Intelligent load balancer for WAF instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.log_service
documentation: :Property: logService: Log service for WAF instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.log_storage
documentation: :Property: logStorage: Log storage capacity.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.period
documentation:
:Property:

period: The subscription period of the firewallIf PeriodUnit is month, the valid range is 1, 3, 6
If periodUnit is year, the valid range is 1, 2, 3

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.period_unit
documentation:
:Property:

periodUnit: The unit of the subscription duration. Valid values:
Month
Year
Default value: Month.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.qps_extension
documentation: :Property: qpsExtension: Extended QPS.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.traffic_billing_protection_threshold
documentation: :Property: trafficBillingProtectionThreshold: In pay-as-you-go WAF 3.0, the traffic billing protection feature is automatically enabled to prevent unexpected and unusually high bills that result from unpredictable factors such as HTTP flood attacks. A bill is not generated for an hour if the peak traffic exceeds the traffic billing protection threshold within the hour. Then, your WAF instance is added to a sandbox. If the peak traffic is lower than the traffic billing protection threshold the next hour, your WAF instance is removed from the sandbox.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosInstanceProps.waf_version
documentation: :Property: wafVersion: The version of WAF3.0.

documentation:
---
param applied_type: true
param certificate_id: true
---

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.CertificatesProperty.applied_type
documentation:
:Property:

appliedType: The certificate type for the HTTPS protocol. Valid values:
default: Indicates the default certificate.
extension: Indicates an extended certificate.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.CertificatesProperty.certificate_id
documentation: :Property: certificateId: The ID of the certificate that was added.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.__init__
documentation:
---
param certificates: true
param cipher_suite: true
param custom_ciphers: true
param enable_tl_sv3: true
param http2_enabled: true
param port: true
param protocol: true
param resource_instance_id: true
param resource_product: true
param tls_version: true
---

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.port
documentation: :Property: port: Access the cloud product port of WAF.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.protocol
documentation: :Property: protocol: Protocol type

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.resource_instance_id
documentation: :Property: resourceInstanceId: The instance ID of the cloud product connected to WAF.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.resource_product
documentation: :Property: resourceProduct: Access to WAF cloud products.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.certificates
documentation: :Property: certificates: The domain name bound to the certificate.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.cipher_suite
documentation:
:Property:

cipherSuite: The type of encryption suite to be added. This parameter is used only when the value of HttpsPorts is not empty (indicating that the domain name uses the HTTPS protocol). Values:
1: Indicates adding all encryption suites.
2: Indicates adding a strong encryption suite. This value can be selected only when the value of TLSVersion is tlsv1.2.
99: Indicates adding a custom encryption suite.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.custom_ciphers
documentation: :Property: customCiphers: Customize the encryption suite list. This parameter is used only when CipherSuite is set to 99.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.enable_tl_sv3
documentation:
:Property:

enableTlSv3: Whether to support TSL1.3 version. This parameter is used only when the value of HttpsPorts is not empty (indicating that the domain name uses the HTTPS protocol). Value:
true: indicates that TSL1.3 version is supported.
false: indicates that TSL1.3 version is not supported.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.http2_enabled
documentation:
:Property:

http2Enabled: Whether to enable HTTP2. This parameter is used only when the value of HttpsPorts is not empty (indicating that the domain name uses the HTTPS protocol). Values:
true: Enable HTTP2.
false (default): Disable HTTP2.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.ListenProperty.tls_version
documentation:
:Property:

tlsVersion: The TLS version to be added. This parameter is used only when the value of HttpsPorts is not empty (indicating that the domain name uses the HTTPS protocol). Values:
tlsv1
tlsv1.1
tlsv1.2

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.__init__
documentation:
---
param keepalive: true
param keepalive_requests: true
param keepalive_timeout: true
param read_timeout: true
param request_headers: true
param write_timeout: true
param xff_header_mode: true
param xff_headers: true
param xff_proto: true
---

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.keepalive
documentation:
:Property:

keepalive: Whether to maintain a long connection. Value:
true (default): indicates to maintain a long connection.
false: indicates not to maintain a long connection.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.keepalive_requests
documentation: :Property: keepaliveRequests: The number of requests for multiplexing long connections. Value range: 60~1000, unit: number.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.keepalive_timeout
documentation: :Property: keepaliveTimeout: Idle long connection timeout, value range: 1~60, default 15, unit: second.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.read_timeout
documentation: :Property: readTimeout: Read timeout duration, unit: seconds. Value range: 1~3600.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.request_headers
documentation: :Property: requestHeaders: The traffic tag field and value of the domain name, used to tag traffic processed by WAF.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.write_timeout
documentation: :Property: writeTimeout: Write timeout duration, unit: seconds. Value range: 1~3600.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.xff_header_mode
documentation:
:Property:

xffHeaderMode: The way WAF obtains the client's real IP. Value:
0 (default): indicates that the client access traffic is not forwarded by other seven-layer proxies before reaching WAF.
1: indicates that WAF reads the first value of the X-Forwarded-For (XFF) field in the request header as the client IP.
2: indicates that WAF reads the custom field value set by you in the request header as the client IP.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.xff_headers
documentation: :Property: xffHeaders: Sets a custom field list for obtaining the client IP, expressed in the format of ["header1", "header2", ...].

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RedirectProperty.xff_proto
documentation:
:Property:

xffProto: X-Forward-For-Proto The protocol of WAF. Value:
true (default): indicates that the protocol of WAF is transmitted.
false: indicates that the protocol of WAF is not transmitted.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RequestHeadersProperty.__init__
documentation:
---
param key: true
param value: true
---

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RequestHeadersProperty.key
documentation: :Property: key: The specified custom request header field.

methods.name: ros_cdk_waf3.RosTGW.RequestHeadersProperty.value
documentation: :Property: value: Customize the value set by the request header field.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::WAF3::TGW`, which is used to add a resource to Web Application Firewall (WAF) in transparent proxy mode.

```
Note

```

This class does not contain additional functions, so it is recommended to use the `TGW` class instead of this class for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-waf3-tgw>

This class encapsulates and extends the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::WAF3::TGW`, which is used to add a resource to Web Application Firewall (WAF) in transparent proxy mode.

This class may have some new functions to facilitate development, so it is recommended to use this class instead of RosTGW\`\`for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-waf3-tgw>
